title: Vietnam Salary Calculator
subtitle: Calculate your net income easily with latest 2024 tax rules
faqDescription: Learn more about Vietnam salary laws and deduction rules.
a1: Every employee in Vietnam is entitled to a personal deduction of 11,000,000 VND per month from their taxable income.
a2: Dependents can include children under 18, students with low income, or elderly parents. Each dependent reduces your taxable income by 4,400,000 VND per month.
a3: Personal Income Tax (PIT) is calculated using a progressive table with rates from 5% to 35% based on your taxable income.
dependentTooltip: Each eligible dependent (children, parents) reduces taxable income by 4.4M VND/month.
grossTooltip: Your total salary before any insurance or tax deductions.
hasNoDirectSupport: Has no other direct support source
q4: How much is the Unemployment Benefit?
a4: The monthly benefit is 60% of your average salary of the last 6 months, capped at 5 times the regional minimum wage.
a5: If an SI participant dies, their family receives 10 times the current base salary for funeral expenses (approx 23.4M VND in 2024).
a6: 3 months for the first 12-36 months of payment, then 1 extra month for every additional 12 months with a maximum of 12 months total.
seoDescription: The Vietnam Salary Calculator (Tinh Tien Luong) helps expats and local residents securely estimate their net income without any data leaving your device. It accurately evaluates modern progressive tax brackets (from 5% to 35%), compulsory insurance deductions like Social Insurance (8%), Health Insurance (1.5%), and Unemployment Insurance (1%), alongside base deductions like the 11M VND personal allowance. Calculate your USD or VND gross salary instantly across monthly, fortnightly, or annual tracking periods.
footerCredit: Built with statically exported Next.js + Vanilla CSS.
